Preview

Pucking Wild: Jacksonville Rays, Book 2 Audible Audiobook – Unabridged

Emily Rath (Author), Alex Kydd (Narrator), Amy Hall (Narrator), Tantor Audio (Publisher) & 1 more
4.4 4.4 out of 5 stars (23,386)
Book 2 of 4: Jacksonville Rays
Best of #BookTok
Sorry, there was a problem loading this page. Try again.
See all formats and editions

My name is Tess Owens, and my life is officially on fire. I never thought dancing at my best friend's wedding would be the match to light the flames. But with one lingering touch, one longing look . . . and one sneaky caterer holding a camera phone, my almost ex-husband is ready to torch my entire life.

Unwilling to face the heat, I do what I do best: I run. Straight into the arms of Rachel and her team of NHL hockey players. One player stands apart from the rest. Jacksonville Rays star forward Ryan Langley is out with an injury. I get the surprise of my life when he shows up at my door, declaring himself my roommate.

Now I have six weeks to launch a new business. Six weeks to fight for my freedom . . . all while fighting my growing attraction to this All-American sweetheart. Did I mention he's ten years younger than me?

I want to believe him when he says age is just a number. I want to believe that our pasts don't define us forever. But first I have to stop running. With one innocent dance, Ryan Langley helped me start this fire. What if we're meant to fan these flames? What if, together, we're meant to burn?

Contains mature themes.

Read more
©2023 Emily Rath Books (P)2023 Tantor

How customer reviews and ratings work

Customer Reviews, including Product Star Ratings help customers to learn more about the product and decide whether it is the right product for them.

To calculate the overall star rating and percentage breakdown by star, we don’t use a simple average. Instead, our system considers things like how recent a review is and if the reviewer bought the item on Amazon. It also analyzed reviews to verify trustworthiness.

Learn more how customers reviews work on Amazon

Customers say

Customers love this hockey romance for its sweet storyline and character development, particularly appreciating the banter between characters. The book is fast-paced and addictive, with customers noting it's fun to read and full of laughter and tears. Customers describe the romance as romantic and heartwarming, with one customer mentioning it's a beautiful story of self-discovery. The spice level receives positive feedback, with customers noting it's spicier than the first book in the series.

Customers enjoy the story quality of the book, describing it as a sweet and perfect tale of two people, with one customer noting it's a beautiful story of self-discovery.
"I cannot get enough of this series!!! Feeling fully sated, it's a great story that brings everything you love from the previous releases...." Read more
"...Although Rachel and her men still hold my heart. This was a great love story and ultimately one about survival and being strong to start over." Read more
"I loved this story! I felt Tess’ worries and happiness! She was one strong B. However, Ryan was such a hoot...." Read more
"...You get more of Rachel and her guys plus an amazing love story that has lots of spice. This is a 5/5 star and 5/5 spice 5/5 plot. GO READ IT NOW!..." Read more
78 customers mention character development, 74 positive, 4 negative78 customers mention "Character development"74 positive4 negative
Customers love the characters in the book, particularly appreciating their personalities and the amazing banter between them.
"Loved this book and this series! Love all the characters and especially Ryan he's the sweetest most thoughtful kind of man...." Read more
"...Tess & Ryan were just the best. I loved their banter and the character development. Both so funny and filled with so much love." Read more
"...I loved the conflict resolution I love the characters I love that you still kept a strong presence of your other charters in this book!..." Read more
"...I can’t get enough of the Jacksonville Rays series. The characters, the love stories, the spice, the friendship….it’s incredible...." Read more

Customers find the book highly readable, describing it as a fast and fun read.
"This was a good read. I enjoyed the characters and story line. I look forward to seeing what is in store next." Read more
"...This book gives you all the feels. Great read. Can’t wait for Poppy’s story" Read more
"I enjoyed this book so much. The narcissist ex mad me want to neck punch him, but other then that character I enjoyed this book so much...." Read more
"...Tess is a kick and Ryan takes your breath away with his sweetness. A fun read!" Read more
65 customers mention romance, 64 positive, 1 negative65 customers mention "Romance"64 positive1 negative
Customers enjoy the romance in the book, describing it as sweet and romantic with a satisfying HEA, and one customer notes it's a hockey romance.
"This book was so good. Dr. Rath does it again. Pucking Wild was funny, sweet, and sometimes I wanted to shake Tess, but she is my spirit animal...." Read more
"...Ryan and Tess are so hot and so sweet, sort of like a good BBQ, and the entire Jacksonville Rays organization gives family a great new meaning...." Read more
"...Love this author!!! Love hockey romance!!" Read more
"...Oh my gosh, that epilogue was like the best thing ever! Ryan is the sweetest, most adorable guy ever and is absolutely perfect for Tess...." Read more
64 customers mention emotional content, 59 positive, 5 negative64 customers mention "Emotional content"59 positive5 negative
Customers appreciate the emotional depth of the book, describing it as heartwarming and tear-jerking, with one customer noting it's steamy with a dash of emotion.
"...It was different and emotional and honestly I'm not a big fan of sequels a lot of them are just disappointing compared to the first however I loved..." Read more
"...This book is raw and vulnerable while also being heartwarming and funny. Highly recommend" Read more
"Stfu. I spent this entire book on an emotional roller coaster. I laughed I cried. This might be one of my favorite books. A+!!" Read more
"Amazing the thought and passion of the very real and raw characters. This was a great book. Love how it built off the 1st book" Read more
62 customers mention pacing, 57 positive, 5 negative62 customers mention "Pacing"57 positive5 negative
Customers enjoy the pacing of the book, finding it addictive with an engaging dynamic between the characters.
"...Ugh, my heart is so full. This was one wild ride and oh so worth it." Read more
"...This book was so good. Angst, drama, wild, hilarious, spice, and so much more. It has growth and it was relatable. I loved this book...." Read more
"...It doesn't matter... they're perfect for each other...." Read more
"...I love Tess and Ryan. I loved there was no third act break up just a woman learning to be brave for herself. 10/10" Read more
61 customers mention spice level, 60 positive, 1 negative61 customers mention "Spice level"60 positive1 negative
Customers enjoy the spicy scenes in the book, finding them more intense than previous installments in the series.
"Love Emily Rath’s writing. This series is well written. Spicy and funny. I can’t wait for more to come in the hockey world she has created." Read more
"...Sobbing here! The story has just a right amount of angst, spice, found family, protectiveness, playfulness and all the freaking feels...." Read more
"I loved Pucking Around. The spice was spicing and the characters are adorable. I was waiting for Pucking Wild because I love Tess...." Read more
"...It follows the story of Tess and Ryan. This book is SPICY. Ryan is a secret freak in the best ways." Read more
55 customers mention humor, 54 positive, 1 negative55 customers mention "Humor"54 positive1 negative
Customers enjoy the book's humor, particularly its witty banter and ability to evoke both laughter and tears.
"...This book was witty, funny, heartbreaking and so much more. For me personally it didn't read as easily as Pucking around but it was still very good...." Read more
"Such a fun and sexy story. I love how the characters in the first book follow along with Tess and Ryan. That being said the heat is hot...." Read more
"I have no words to describe how amazing this book is. It made me laugh and cry and made me just feel so good all over to read...." Read more
"...The two shouldn’t go together, but Emily proves us wrong! This witty, EXPLOSIVE, and surprising book is heavier than the last, but it’s done..." Read more

Amazon Customer
5 out of 5 stars
Tess and Ryan are Perfect! 😍
🏒 Accidental Roommates to Lovers 🏒 Friends with Benefits 🏒 Hockey Romance 🏒 Reverse Age Gap 🏒 He Falls First 🏒 Plus Size Rep 🏒 Bisexual Rep 🏒 Praise / Dom(me)/Sub 🏒 Toy Use 🏒 Taylor Swift References ⚠ Narcissistic and Abusive Ex This is the 2nd book in the Jacksonville Rays series and focuses on Tess Owens and Ryan Langley. Tess escapes to Jacksonville to get away from her ex and to help Mars with his sea turtle nonprofit. Ryan gets injured and needs to rest and recuperate at a place without stairs. Due to miscommunication, both Tess and Ryan end up staying at Mars’ old house at the same time. They have both been interested in each other since that fateful beach day months before and when they met again recently at their friends’ wedding. Sparks start flying again and this time they don’t know if they can stay away. Holy rollercoaster of emotions! Dr. Emily freaking Rath did it again with this second installment! This book exceeded my expectations (which were already high!) and I just fell in love with the story! Tess and Ryan are just PERFECT! Their relationship is magnetizing, honest, and real. Plus, all the SPICE! 🥵🌶 Ryan is my little hockey puppy dog (who is such a good boy) with a newly discovered dominant side. Tess is my wild and loving redhead with just a shitstorm of a personal life. She’s also my spirit animal. Her ex has put her through literal hell and she’s just trying to drag herself out and be free. I 𝘳𝘦𝘢𝘭𝘭𝘺 hated the ex and I haven’t felt this strong about a character in a while. I use to live in the Jacksonville area in college and I really loved all the continued JAX references because I actually knew about the mentioned locations and agencies. Felt drawn into the story and series even more because of that. We do see quite a bit of Rachel, Jake, Caleb, and Mars in this book and I just can’t get enough of them. While this book 𝘤𝘢𝘯 be read as an interconnected standalone, it’s absolutely best read after reading the first. Jake is still Jake in this book and I cackled with his metaphor use. IYKYK. We also get glimpses of what happens in the next book, Pucking Sweet, and I’m so excited for that story!

Top reviews from the United States

There was a problem filtering reviews. Please reload the page.

  • Christina (christinareadsbooks)
    5.0 out of 5 stars Emily has done it again!!!!
    Reviewed in the United States on August 21, 2023
    Format: KindleVerified Purchase
    Emily has done it again, y'all 👏🏻🙌🏻 She gave Tess "Goddess" Owens and Ryan "Puppy" Langley their perfect story. This book is not just the story of how they fell in love, but its about how Tess became her true self. With the help of her friends and her makeshift family, she healed old wounds and started living life the way she wanted to.

    *Pucking Wild starts in December when Rachel, Jake, Caleb, and Ilmari get married. So the Pucking Wild timeline is between the ending and the epilogue of Pucking Around*

    ⬇️ Tropes ⬇️
    🏒 Hockey
    🏒 Reverse age gap
    🏒 Close Proximity
    🏒 Friends to lovers
    🏒 hurt/comfort
    🏒 Dom/Domme switch
    🏒 Found family
    🏒 Plus size FMC
    🏒 Semi slow burn 🔥
    🏒 P🥚ing
    🏒 🍑 play
    🏒 He falls first
    🏒 No 3rd act breakup
    🏒 Toxic ex

    As soon as this hit my Kindle, I immediately started reading it! Within the first 8% of the book, I was crying, lol. Seeing Rachel and her boys get married was beautiful 🥰 I love the friendship that Tess and Rachel have. Really, Tess's friendship with Rachel is her strongest relationship that she's had in her life. She has never been a priority to her family or her ex and his family, and that has caused some deep emotional scars.

    I dont want to go into a lot of details about the plot because I dont want to spoil anything, and I want your emotions about certain plot points to come organically. I will say that Tess ends up going to Jacksonville to help Ilmari with his non-profit sea turtle rescue. I really loved seeing Tess and Ilmari's friendship grow. Ilamri is a man of few words, but the words he does speak are always profound.

    “Because life is short, Ryan. Life is beautiful and short, and you only get one. Live it on purpose.” -Ilmari

    Ryan plays in the Winter Classics and gets a knee injury. His apartment has stairs, so Jake says he can use Ilmari's house why he's rehabbing his knee..... little does Jake know that Rachel told Tess she could stay at Ilmari's. I love a close proximity trope!!! Tess and Ryan have off the charts chemistry, so we know its only a matter of time until they give in to their connection.

    This book is more emotionally heavy than Pucking Around was. Tess has so much baggage and deep trauma that she has to work through. It was honestly beautiful reading it all unfold. Ryan is such a positive influence in her life. Honestly, all of her Jacksonville friends are. They rally around her and lift her up when she can't see an end in sight.

    Catching up with everyone from the Rays was like coming home after months of being away. I love this group of people so much! All of the little Easter eggs about Poppy, Marrow, and Novy were so good! Im dying for Pucking Sweet to come out! I know it is going to be a mess 🤣 but a mess in the best way possible!

    If you love the Jacksonville Ray's universe, then you probably already love Tess and Ryan, but if you needed more confirmation, lol... read Pucking Wild! It's funny, emotional, spicy, and everything good you could want in a book! Thank you Emily, for giving us this amazing series!

    Reading order
    #1 That One Night
    #2 Pucking Around
    #3 Pucking Ever After vol. 1
    #4 Pucking Wild
    Read more
    8 people found this helpful
    Helpful
     Report
  • OutsideOfTheTrackBooks
    4.0 out of 5 stars Good, But Missing a Bit of the Magic.
    Reviewed in the United States on October 19, 2025
    Format: KindleVerified Purchase
    I loved Pucking Around, and I liked this one—but it didn’t quite hit the same. Ryan was great, but Tess felt a bit much at times, almost repetitive. Normally I’m not a big fan of reverse age gaps, but it actually worked fine here.

    What this book really missed for me was a funny character. The first one had that perfect balance of humor and heart, and this one just felt a little more serious. Still, I liked the story, the world, and the writing overall.

    The narrators did a great job, and I’d definitely read it again.
    Read more

    5.0 out of 5 stars Tess and Ryan are Perfect! 😍
    Reviewed in the United States on August 21, 2023
    Format: KindleVerified Purchase
    🏒 Accidental Roommates to Lovers
    🏒 Friends with Benefits
    🏒 Hockey Romance
    🏒 Reverse Age Gap
    🏒 He Falls First
    🏒 Plus Size Rep
    🏒 Bisexual Rep

    ⚠ Narcissistic and Abusive Ex

    This is the 2nd book in the Jacksonville Rays series and focuses on Tess Owens and Ryan Langley. Tess escapes to Jacksonville to get away from her ex and to help Mars with his sea turtle nonprofit. Ryan gets injured and needs to rest and recuperate at a place without stairs. Due to miscommunication, both Tess and Ryan end up staying at Mars’ old house at the same time. They have both been interested in each other since that fateful beach day months before and when they met again recently at their friends’ wedding. Sparks start flying again and this time they don’t know if they can stay away.

    Holy rollercoaster of emotions! Dr. Emily freaking Rath did it again with this second installment! This book exceeded my expectations (which were already high!) and I just fell in love with the story! Tess and Ryan are just PERFECT! Their relationship is magnetizing, honest, and real. Plus, all the SPICE! 🥵🌶 Ryan is my little hockey puppy dog (who is such a good boy) with a newly discovered dominant side. Tess is my wild and loving redhead with just a shitstorm of a personal life. She’s also my spirit animal. Her ex has put her through literal hell and she’s just trying to drag herself out and be free. I 𝘳𝘦𝘢𝘭𝘭𝘺 hated the ex and I haven’t felt this strong about a character in a while. I use to live in the Jacksonville area in college and I really loved all the continued JAX references because I actually knew about the mentioned locations and agencies. Felt drawn into the story and series even more because of that.

    We do see quite a bit of Rachel, Jake, Caleb, and Mars in this book and I just can’t get enough of them. While this book 𝘤𝘢𝘯 be read as an interconnected standalone, it’s absolutely best read after reading the first. Jake is still Jake in this book and I cackled with his metaphor use. IYKYK. We also get glimpses of what happens in the next book, Pucking Sweet, and I’m so excited for that story!

    ⚠ Narcissistic and Abusive Ex

    This is the 2nd book in the Jacksonville Rays series and focuses on Tess Owens and Ryan Langley. Tess escapes to Jacksonville to get away from her ex and to help Mars with his sea turtle nonprofit. Ryan gets injured and needs to rest and recuperate at a place without stairs. Due to miscommunication, both Tess and Ryan end up staying at Mars’ old house at the same time. They have both been interested in each other since that fateful beach day months before and when they met again recently at their friends’ wedding. Sparks start flying again and this time they don’t know if they can stay away.

    Holy rollercoaster of emotions! Dr. Emily freaking Rath did it again with this second installment! This book exceeded my expectations (which were already high!) and I just fell in love with the story! Tess and Ryan are just PERFECT! Their relationship is magnetizing, honest, and real. Plus, all the SPICE! 🥵🌶 Ryan is my little hockey puppy dog (who is such a good boy) with a newly discovered dominant side. Tess is my wild and loving redhead with just a shitstorm of a personal life. She’s also my spirit animal. Her ex has put her through literal hell and she’s just trying to drag herself out and be free. I 𝘳𝘦𝘢𝘭𝘭𝘺 hated the ex and I haven’t felt this strong about a character in a while. I use to live in the Jacksonville area in college and I really loved all the continued JAX references because I actually knew about the mentioned locations and agencies. Felt drawn into the story and series even more because of that.

    We do see quite a bit of Rachel, Jake, Caleb, and Mars in this book and I just can’t get enough of them. While this book 𝘤𝘢𝘯 be read as an interconnected standalone, it’s absolutely best read after reading the first. Jake is still Jake in this book and I cackled with his metaphor use. IYKYK. We also get glimpses of what happens in the next book, Pucking Sweet, and I’m so excited for that story!
    Images in this review
    2 people found this helpful
    Helpful

Plus size representation in this emotionally charged, spicy romance with complex characters navigating personal growth and unconventional relationships. Golden retriever M/F

  • Phoebe Walker-Gubbins
    5.0 out of 5 stars THANK YOU EMILY RATH <3 Another 5 Stars Read!
    Reviewed in the United Kingdom on January 7, 2024
    Format: KindleVerified Purchase
    My, oh my. I do not know how Emily Rath has written such an amazing, and long, book in such a small time. I am SO HERE for the writing style - it is so smooth and unique, so easy for me to read. It flows so well in my thoughts and it comes off as witty and humorous, as well as intelligent and flirty. The character development for both Tess and Ryan in this story - but also Ilmari and other strong characters from the previous books - completely reignite my love for the Jacksonville Rays universe. Emily Rath is a total wizard at writing these hockey romances and I feel so lucky to have found such an amazing author! I loved and cried with the characters in this book, as I did with the others before and most likely will with the next books. Tess and Ryan are so likeable and their characteristics, traits etc are so well developed. I found Tess so relatable, to the extent where this book felt both inspirational and healing to me.

    Emily writes with such passion, such extravagant and witty wording and all the positive spirit in the world. I really enjoyed us glimpsing back into the Price family, as well as all the setting up for the books to come. I already love Poppy based on what we have seen of her in this book - and to be honest, would even be more than happy to have a book about Shelby. It is another absolute hit for me - another 5 star read I highly recommend - especially if you love hockey romance, reverse age gap, a kickass, independent female lead and a sweet puppy male lead. The tension and steam was of course perfect in all the right places, too, but the plot and growth of the characters was something entirely incredible on its own as well.

    I could go on about this book and praise it forever, truly. Thank-you for sharing Tess' story with us and letting us be a part of this story, Emily! I can't wait to revisit this book and the others in the rays universe again already.
    Read more
